hero.title: A cinematic BaZi and astrology experience for the modern seeker
hero.description: Modern Mystic turns birth data into a multi-layered reading journey: test entry, result page, checkout flow, and premium destiny report, all wrapped in a more intentional visual language.
hero.tip: Legacy chart flow remains available while the native ShipAny experience is being rebuilt.

introduce.title: What Modern Mystic is rebuilding inside ShipAny
introduce.label: Core Experience
introduce.description: This project is no longer just a static landing site. It is being rebuilt as a real ShipAny application so the built-in admin, payment settings, and content controls can manage the website from one place.

items.title: Chart Entry Flow
items.description: Users can enter date, time, and location, then move straight into the result reading flow.

items.title: Result and Premium Narrative
items.description: The result page and premium report carry the dark celestial editorial style you already designed.

items.title: Knowledge Hub
items.description: Educational BaZi and astrology content remains accessible as a first-class part of the site.

items.title: Admin-Controlled Settings
items.description: Payment providers, app settings, and configurable modules now belong to ShipAny's native admin system.

benefits.title: Why this migration matters
benefits.label: Platform Shift
benefits.description: The goal is not just to copy pages into a new template. The goal is to move Modern Mystic onto a framework that already understands admin permissions, payments, configurable settings, and structured growth.

items.title: Native Admin Backend
items.description: Instead of maintaining a custom admin panel, Modern Mystic can use ShipAny's own admin settings, payment pages, and RBAC system.

items.title: Safer Payment Foundation
items.description: PayPal and other providers are already modeled in ShipAny's settings and payment service layer.

items.title: Expandable Product Structure
items.description: The site can grow from a chart calculator into a full SaaS-style content and reading platform without rewriting the foundation again.

features.title: Migration priorities
features.description: The first ShipAny version focuses on preserving the existing user journey while moving control into a more durable system.

items.title: Keep the legacy chart flow alive
items.description: Existing result, premium report, and chart-entry pages stay available during migration.

items.title: Move brand and navigation first
items.description: Header, footer, homepage messaging, and overall identity are aligned before deeper page rewrites.

items.title: Use ShipAny settings for payments
items.description: PayPal configuration is read from ShipAny's settings layer instead of a custom JSON admin.

items.title: Prepare for native page rewrites
items.description: Legacy pages can gradually become real Next routes after the platform baseline is stable.

faq.title: Migration FAQ
faq.description: A few practical answers about what is happening in this first phase.

items.title: Will the old pages disappear immediately?
items.description: No. The existing chart pages remain available during migration so the experience does not break while the ShipAny-native version is being rebuilt.

items.title: Will the ShipAny admin replace the custom admin?
items.description: Yes. The plan is to rely on ShipAny's own admin settings and permission system rather than keeping a separate custom dashboard.

items.title: Is PayPal still part of the flow?
items.description: Yes. The migration keeps PayPal in scope, but its configuration is meant to be controlled through ShipAny settings.

items.title: Why not rebuild every page at once?
items.description: Because the safest migration path is to establish the platform foundation first, then move each functional page one by one without breaking the live reading flow.

cta.title: Enter the live chart flow
cta.description: Use the current chart entry while the ShipAny-native Modern Mystic experience continues to take shape behind the scenes.
